gold_responses.title: Missing Stand Components
gold_responses.scenario_summary: Customer reports missing hardware or components needed for assembly
gold_responses.full_text:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

That's certainly not how your order should have arrived. If you're able to send through a couple of photos of what you received, I'll take a look straight away and organise the correct replacement parts for you.

We'll get this sorted as quickly as possible.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
why_high_quality: Clear next step (photos) before taking action
Warm, personal sign-off consistent with brand
mustInclude: Validation that arrival was not as expected
Request for photos to identify missing parts
mustAvoid: Promising specific dispatch timeline before seeing photos

retrieval_metadata.summary: Requests photos to quickly replace missing hardware or components from a new delivery.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer reports they cannot finish assembly because pieces are missing from the delivery.

gold_responses.title: Box vs Drawer Design Intent
gold_responses.scenario_summary: Customer asks about gluing boxes or complains boxes are hard to open
gold_responses.full_text:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you so much for your message.

The first thing to note is that the Sagitine Boxes are designed as storage boxes, not drawers. They sit within the frame rather than on runners, which allows you to remove, reorganise, and protect your pieces more effectively over time.

Because of this, they are intentionally a more considered interaction. Many customers find it becomes very natural quite quickly, particularly when using two hands to open the Boxes.

I hope that context is helpful, and of course please reach out if you have any other questions.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
why_high_quality: Explains design intent (storage boxes vs drawers)
Highlights premium construction to justify advice
mustInclude: Tip to use two hands for the interaction
mustAvoid: Saying the product is 'supposed to be hard to open'

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains that Sagitine Boxes are meant to be removed completely and are not traditional sliding drawers.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when a customer complains about boxes being difficult to slide in and out or asks for advice on operation.

gold_responses.title: No Return Required for Damaged Paper Boxes
gold_responses.scenario_summary: Damaged paper-based boxes replaced without requiring return
gold_responses.full_text:
Hi [Customer Name],

That's no trouble at all.

There is no need to return the damaged Boxes. If you're able to find another use for them, that would be lovely, and if not, please feel free to recycle them.

I'll arrange the refund to the same payment method used for your order once confirmed.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
why_high_quality: Reduces friction by not demanding return of low-salvage item
Clearly states resolution and refund process
mustInclude: Confirmation that return is not required
mustAvoid: Demanding return of damaged paper boxes

applicability.risk_levels: Low (customer is relieved by no-return policy)

retrieval_metadata.summary: Confirms damaged paper-based Boxes do not need to be returned and offers refund.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when paper-based Boxes arrive damaged and customer asks about returning them.

gold_responses.title: Refund Instead of Replacement
gold_responses.scenario_summary: Customer prefers refund over replacement for faulty/damaged item
gold_responses.full_text:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your message.

That's absolutely fine. I'll arrange your refund back to the same payment method used for your order. It typically takes around 3 business days to appear, although some banks can take up to 10.

Please just confirm that you're happy for me to process it that way.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
why_high_quality: Accepts customer preference without pushing back
mustAvoid: Pushing back to convince them to take replacement

retrieval_metadata.summary: Confirms a customer's request for a refund instead of a replacement, outlining standard banking timelines.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer explicitly asks for a refund after a fault/issue is identified, rather than requesting new parts.

templates.title: Lost in Transit - Investigation
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for reaching out about your delivery.

I can see that tracking hasn't updated for some time. Let me investigate this with the courier for you straight away.

I'll follow up with them and get back to you within 24 hours with an update.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Composed, proactive, taking ownership without over-apologising

retrieval_metadata.summary: Initiates courier investigation when tracking shows no movement beyond expected delivery window.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when tracking hasn't updated for more than 3 days beyond expected delivery date.

templates.title: Marked Delivered But Not Received
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for letting me know.

I can see that tracking shows as delivered, though I understand you haven't received your package. Let me look into this for you.

Could you please check if it might have been left in a safe spot, with a neighbour, or at a nearby collection point? Sometimes couriers leave a calling card with details.

If you're still unable to locate it, please let me know and I'll raise an investigation with the courier.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Helpful and systematic, not dismissive of customer's claim
mustInclude: Practical suggestions for where it might be
Commitment to investigate if not found

retrieval_metadata.summary: Guides customer to check nearby locations before initiating courier investigation for marked-as-delivered packages.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when tracking shows 'Delivered' but customer claims they did not receive the package.

templates.title: Stolen After Delivery
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for letting me know.

I'm so sorry to hear that your package was taken after delivery. I understand how frustrating that must be.

Let me raise a claim with the courier and also provide you with a police report number that may help with any insurance claim you wish to make.

I'll be in touch within 24-48 hours with next steps.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Empathetic while maintaining professional distance, takes ownership of resolution
mustInclude: Offer of police report number support

retrieval_metadata.summary: Handles theft-after-delivery reports with courier claim and police report support.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer confirms package was stolen from their property after delivery.

templates.title: Wrong Address Entered by Customer
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for reaching out.

I can see that the order was shipped to the address provided at checkout. Unfortunately, once an order has been dispatched, I'm not able to redirect it in transit.

If the package is returned to us as undeliverable, I'll be in touch to arrange reshipment to the correct address.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Clear and factual, not accusatory, sets expectations appropriately
mustInclude: Confirmation of what address was used
Explanation that redirection is not possible in transit
What happens if package is returned
mustAvoid: Promising they can intercept the package

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains that orders shipped to incorrect addresses cannot be redirected in transit.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer realises they entered the wrong address and order has already been dispatched.

templates.title: Courier Delay Beyond Expected Window
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for checking in on your order.

I can see that your package is in transit with the courier, though it appears to be moving a little more slowly than expected. This sometimes happens during particularly busy periods.

Your estimated delivery is now [updated timeframes]. I'll continue monitoring your tracking and will reach out if there are any further updates.

Thank you for your patience.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Reassuring and transparent, not defensive about courier performance
mustInclude: Confirmation that package is still in transit

retrieval_metadata.summary: Provides updated delivery estimates when courier delays occur beyond expected window.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when tracking shows movement but package is beyond estimated delivery date.

templates.title: Partial Delivery - Missing Carton
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for letting me know.

I can see that your order was shipped in multiple packages. It looks like the second carton is still on its way to you.

The tracking for the remaining package is [tracking link]. Your expected delivery for that carton is [timeframe].

If you don't receive it by [date], please let me know and I'll investigate further.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Clear and reassuring, provides concrete information
mustInclude: Confirmation that order was split into multiple packages
mustAvoid: Assuming it's lost without checking tracking
Making customer feel anxious about the missing package

applicability.risk_levels: Low (other carton is still in transit)

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains partial deliveries and provides tracking for remaining cartons.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer receives only part of their order and other cartons are still in transit.

templates.title: Just Missed Sale / Offer
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your message.

I understand you're disappointed to have just missed our recent promotion. Unfortunately, I'm not able to apply discount codes retrospectively to orders that have already been placed.

We do run promotions throughout the year, so I'd recommend keeping an eye on your email for future offers.

I hope you understand, and thank you for shopping with Sagitine.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Firm but kind, not robotic, acknowledges disappointment without conceding
mustInclude: Clear explanation that retrospective discounts are not possible

retrieval_metadata.summary: Declines requests to apply recently-ended promotions to existing orders.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks to apply a discount code that just expired to their recent order.

templates.title: Discount Code Failed
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for reaching out.

I'm sorry to hear that the discount code didn't work at checkout. Let me help troubleshoot this.

Could you please confirm:
- The exact code you tried to use
- Whether any error message appeared
- The type of discount (percentage, fixed amount, free shipping)

Once I have those details, I'll be able to look into what went wrong and get this sorted for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Helpful and troubleshooting-oriented, not dismissive
mustInclude: Request for specific details to troubleshoot

retrieval_metadata.summary: Troubleshoots failed discount codes by requesting details to investigate and resolve.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer reports that a discount code failed at checkout.

templates.title: Free Shipping Request (Below Threshold)
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your message.

I understand you'd like free shipping added to your order. Unfortunately, our free shipping threshold is [amount], and I'm not able to adjust the shipping cost for orders below that amount.

We do regularly offer free shipping promotions, so I'd recommend keeping an eye on your email for those opportunities.

Thank you for your understanding.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Polite and clear, not defensive about the policy
mustInclude: Clear statement of free shipping threshold
mustAvoid: Suggesting ways to game the system

retrieval_metadata.summary: Declines requests to add free shipping to orders below the threshold amount.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks for free shipping on an order below the free shipping threshold.

templates.title: Component Separation - Buying Boxes Separately
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your interest in our products.

I understand you're looking to purchase additional Boxes for your Stand. At the moment, we don't offer components for individual sale. Our pieces are designed as complete systems, and we've found that they work best when used together as intended.

I'm happy to discuss full system options if you're considering expanding your storage setup.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Clear about design philosophy, not dismissive of customer need
mustInclude: Clear statement that components are not sold separately
mustAvoid: Making customer feel unreasonable for asking

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains that Sagitine products are sold as complete systems, not individual components.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks to purchase individual Boxes or Stands separately from complete systems.

templates.title: Frustrated Customer - De-escalation
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for sharing your experience with me.

I can hear how frustrated you are, and I want to make this right. Let me understand what's happened so I can find the best solution for you.

Could you help me by confirming [specific detail needed]? Once I have that information, I'll be able to move forward with resolving this for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Validating and calm, takes ownership without being defensive
mustInclude: Request for specific information needed to resolve
mustAvoid: Making promises that can't be kept

applicability.risk_levels: High (customer may leave negative review or escalate further)

retrieval_metadata.summary: De-escalates frustrated customers by validating emotions and committing to resolution.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer language indicates frustration, anger, or escalation (e.g., multiple emails, exclamation marks, threat language).

templates.title: Product Doesn't Meet Expectations
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for sharing your honest feedback with me.

I'm sorry to hear that the [Box Name] hasn't met your expectations. I understand how disappointing that feels when you've been looking forward to something.

I'd value the opportunity to understand more about what didn't align with what you were expecting. This kind of feedback helps us continually improve our products and communication.

Would you be open to sharing a bit more detail about what specifically didn't meet your expectations?

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Genuinely curious, not defensive, positions feedback as valuable improvement data
mustInclude: Positioning feedback as helpful for improvement
mustAvoid: Trying to 'sell' them on why they should like it

retrieval_metadata.summary: Requests detailed feedback when product doesn't meet customer expectations.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer expresses that product doesn't match expectations or photos.

templates.title: Why Boxes Are Not Drawers - Design Philosophy
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your question about the design.

Sagitine Boxes are intentionally designed as storage boxes rather than drawers. This design choice allows you to:
- Remove Boxes completely to access contents
- Reorganise your storage as your needs change
- Protect pieces by keeping them enclosed when not in use
- Stack and store Boxes separately from the Stand

Unlike drawers on runners, this approach gives you more flexibility in how you use and organise your pieces over time.

I hope that helps explain the thinking behind the design!

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Educational and enthusiastic about design intent, not defensive
mustInclude: Clear explanation of storage box vs drawer concept
mustAvoid: Acting as if customer should already know this
Suggesting modifications to make them more drawer-like

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains the design philosophy behind why Sagitine products are boxes, not drawers.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks why Boxes don't slide like traditional drawers.

templates.title: Why Fit Can Feel Tight - Precision Engineering
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your question about the fit.

Sagitine Boxes are designed with a precise fit within the Stand frame. This intentional tightness serves several purposes:
- Prevents Boxes from shifting during normal use
- Maintains the clean, seamless aesthetic
- Ensures Boxes sit securely without wobbling

When new, the fit can feel particularly snug. This typically relaxes slightly with regular use as the Boxes settle into the frame.

Using two hands to remove Boxes - one to stabilise the Stand and one to lift the Box - makes the interaction much smoother.

I hope that helps!

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Informative and reassuring, not dismissive of the friction concern
mustInclude: Explanation that tight fit is intentional
Reassurance that it settles with use
mustAvoid: Saying the product is 'supposed to be difficult'
Implying customer is using it wrong

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains that tight Box fit is intentional for stability and aesthetics.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer complains that Boxes are hard to remove or fit feels too tight.

templates.title: Dust Protection and Airflow Benefits
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your question about dust protection.

Sagitine Boxes are designed as enclosed storage boxes rather than open drawers, which provides excellent dust protection for your pieces. When the Box is sitting in the Stand, the minimal gap between the Box and frame is intentional - it allows for:
- Air circulation to prevent moisture buildup
- Easy removal without friction
- Flexibility to remove Boxes completely when needed

For pieces that are being stored long-term, you might also consider using garment bags within the Boxes for an extra layer of protection.

I hope that helps with your storage planning!

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Helpful and solution-oriented, not defensive about the design
mustInclude: Acknowledgement that enclosed design protects from dust
Explanation of why gap exists (airflow, removal)
Practical suggestion for long-term storage (garment bags)
mustAvoid: Suggesting modifications to close the gap

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains the balance between dust protection and airflow in the enclosed box design.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks about dust protection or notices the gap between Box and frame.

templates.title: Paper-Based Construction - Care and Limitations
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your question about the Box construction.

Sagitine Boxes are crafted from a premium paper-based, hand-wrapped material. This construction gives them their distinctive aesthetic and lightweight feel, but it does mean they require some specific care:

- Avoid using water or liquid cleaners (wipe clean with a dry, soft cloth)
- Don't use adhesive, tape, or glue on the Boxes (this can damage the paper surface)
- Keep away from direct heat sources or high humidity
- For timber components, use food-safe timber oil quarterly to maintain the finish

This premium construction is part of what gives Sagitine pieces their character, and with proper care they'll serve you beautifully for years to come.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Proud of the premium construction while being clear about care requirements
mustAvoid: Being apologetic about the material choice

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains paper-based construction characteristics and necessary care guidelines.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks about cleaning, materials, or whether they can modify/adhere Boxes.

templates.title: Missing Assembly Instructions
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for reaching out.

I'm sorry to hear that the assembly instructions weren't in your package. Let me get those to you straight away.

I've emailed the instruction guide to [email on file]. You should receive it within the next few minutes.

If you have any questions once you've reviewed the guide, please don't hesitate to ask. Assembly is straightforward and typically takes 5-10 minutes with the included Allen key.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Helpful and proactive, not defensive about the missing instructions
mustAvoid: Making customer feel like they lost them
Over-explaining the assembly process in email

retrieval_metadata.summary: Immediately emails assembly instructions when they were missing from the package.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer reports that assembly instructions were not in the box.

templates.title: Replacement Parts Request
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for reaching out about replacement parts.

I'd be happy to help you with this. To ensure I send you the correct replacement, could you please:

1. Confirm which part you need (e.g., Box, Stand component, hardware, Allen key)
2. Provide a photo if possible (this helps me identify the exact piece)
3. Share your order number if you have it handy

Once I have those details, I'll organise the replacement to be shipped to you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Helpful and systematic, not assuming what part is needed
mustAvoid: Assuming what part they need without clarification
Promising immediate shipping before identifying correct part
Charging for replacement parts (unless clearly outside warranty)

retrieval_metadata.summary: Gathers information to identify and ship correct replacement parts.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer requests replacement parts but doesn't specify exactly what they need.

templates.title: Large Item Return - Pickup Conditions
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for letting me know you'd like to return your Stand.

To arrange the most suitable return pickup, I'll need to confirm a couple of details:

1. Is the Stand currently disassembled, or is it still assembled?
2. Do you still have the original packaging?

Once I have this information, I can book the appropriate courier pickup for you.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Organised and systematic, gathering information needed for smooth return
mustAvoid: Booking pickup without understanding state of item
Making customer responsible for finding packaging

retrieval_metadata.summary: Gathers information about assembly and packaging status to book appropriate return pickup for large items.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer wants to return a Stand or other large item that requires courier pickup.

templates.title: Click and Collect Not Available
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your message.

I understand you'd prefer to pick up your order, but unfortunately we don't currently offer a click-and-collect option. Our Sydney warehouse isn't set up for customer pickups or public access.

All orders are shipped directly to your chosen delivery address. Standard shipping typically takes 5-7 business days, or express shipping is available at checkout if you need your items sooner.

I hope that helps, and thank you for your understanding.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Clear and polite, not defensive about the limitation
mustInclude: Clear statement that click-and-collect is not available
mustAvoid: Being apologetic about the operational reality

retrieval_metadata.summary: Explains that click-and-collect is not available due to warehouse operational constraints.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer (especially Sydney-based) asks if they can pick up their order.

templates.title: Back in Stock / Waitlist
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for your interest in [Box Name].

I'm pleased to let you know that [Box Name] is back in stock and available for purchase on our website now.

Given that you've been waiting for this item, I'd recommend placing your order soon to secure it, as stock is moving quickly.

If you have any questions about the product or your order, please don't hesitate to ask.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Helpful and encouraging, not pressuring but creating gentle urgency
mustInclude: Confirmation that item is back in stock

retrieval_metadata.summary: Notifies waitlist customers that previously out-of-stock items are now available.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when notifying customers that items they were waiting for are back in stock.

templates.title: Preorder Stock Timing Updates
templates.body_template:
Hi [Customer Name],

Thank you for checking in on your preorder.

I'm writing to update you on the timeline for [Box Name]. We're currently expecting this to ship by [revised date], though please understand this is an estimate and may shift slightly as production completes.

I completely understand that waiting is frustrating, and I appreciate your patience. If this revised timeline doesn't work for you, please let me know and I can discuss options.

I'll email you again as soon as your order is dispatched.

Warm regards,
Heidi x
templates.tone_notes: Transparent and empathetic, not defensive about delays
mustInclude: Acknowledgement that this is an estimate
Empathy for the frustration of waiting
Option to cancel if timeline doesn't work

applicability.risk_levels: Medium (customer may cancel if wait is too long)

retrieval_metadata.summary: Provides preorder timeline updates with cancellation options if delays are excessive.
retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when proactively updating preorder customers about delays or responding to timeline inquiries.

policy_rules.description: All damaged or faulty items are replaced free of charge. Photo evidence required before replacement ships. No return shipping required for damaged items.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When customer reports damage, fault, or missing components from delivery.
policy_rules.constraints: Must receive photo evidence before shipping replacement. Customer keeps damaged item.
examples: Please provide a photo of the damage, and we'll arrange a replacement to be shipped within 2 business days.
You don't need to return the original item.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer reports any product damage, fault, or delivery issue.

policy_rules.description: The Sydney warehouse is not equipped for public pickups. All orders must be shipped.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When a customer asks to pick up an order locally in Sydney to save time or shipping costs.
examples: We don't currently offer a click-and-collect option, as our Sydney warehouse isn't set up for customer pickups.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when any customer (especially Sydney-based) asks about local pickup.

policy_rules.rule_name: No Return for Damaged Paper Boxes
policy_rules.description: If Sagitine paper-based boxes arrive substantially damaged, do not require the customer to ship them back.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When replacing or refunding damaged insert boxes.
policy_rules.constraints: Applies to hand-wrapped paper boxes, not necessarily stands/hardware frames.
examples: There is no need to return the damaged Boxes. If you're able to find another use for them, that would be lovely, and if not, please feel free to recycle them.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when paper-based Boxes arrive damaged and customer asks about returning them.

policy_rules.description: Refunds take approximately 3 business days, but explicitly warn that some banks take up to 10.
policy_rules.constraints: Always mention the extended bank timeline to manage finality and expectations.
examples: It typically takes around 3 business days to appear, although some banks can take up to 10.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use whenever explaining when a refund will appear in customer's account.

policy_rules.description: Sagitine products are sold as complete systems. Components are not generally sold separately.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When a customer asks to purchase a single box for a stand, or a stand without boxes.
policy_rules.constraints: Occasional exceptions exist for 'Stand-only' purchases if customer value makes sense, but the default brand stance is 'complete systems'.
examples: At the moment, we don't have plans to offer that piece separately. Our pieces are designed as complete systems.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks to purchase individual components separately from complete systems.

policy_rules.description: Return collections for stands require knowing if the unit is disassembled and if it is in original packaging.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When organizing a courier pickup for a returned stand.
policy_rules.constraints: Must verify state before booking the pickup courier.
examples: I'll just need to confirm whether the Stand is disassembled and whether you still have the original packaging, as that will determine the most suitable pickup method.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer wants to return a Stand or other large item requiring courier pickup.

policy_rules.description: When tracking shows no movement beyond expected delivery window, initiate courier investigation before offering replacement.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When tracking hasn't updated for more than 3 days beyond expected delivery date.
policy_rules.constraints: Must allow 24-48 hours for courier investigation before determining next steps.
examples: Let me investigate this with the courier for you straight away. I'll follow up with them and get back to you within 24 hours with an update.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when tracking shows no movement for 3+ days beyond expected delivery.

policy_rules.description: When tracking shows 'Delivered' but customer claims they did not receive, guide customer to check nearby locations before initiating investigation.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When customer claims non-receipt despite tracking showing delivered status.
policy_rules.constraints: Must first ask customer to check safe spots, neighbours, collection points. Only escalate to investigation if not found.
examples: Could you please check if it might have been left in a safe spot, with a neighbour, or at a nearby collection point?

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when tracking shows 'Delivered' but customer claims they did not receive package.

policy_rules.description: When customer confirms package was stolen after delivery, raise courier claim and provide police report support.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When customer has evidence package was stolen (security footage, witnessed theft).
policy_rules.constraints: Do not admit liability. Courier claim and police report required for insurance. Resolution depends on courier investigation outcome.
examples: Let me raise a claim with the courier and also provide you with a police report number that may help with any insurance claim you wish to make.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer confirms package was stolen from their property after delivery.

policy_rules.rule_name: Wrong Address - Cannot Redirect in Transit
policy_rules.description: Once an order has been dispatched, it cannot be redirected to a different address. Customer must wait for return as undeliverable.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When customer realises they entered wrong address and order has already been dispatched.
policy_rules.constraints: If order hasn't shipped yet, address can be updated. Once shipped, no redirection possible.
examples: Unfortunately, once an order has been dispatched, I'm not able to redirect it in transit. If the package is returned to us as undeliverable, I'll be in touch to arrange reshipment.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer realises they entered wrong address and order has already been dispatched.

policy_rules.description: Promotional codes cannot be applied retrospectively to orders that have already been placed.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When customer asks to apply a discount code that just expired to their recent order.
policy_rules.constraints: No exceptions. This would set precedent and create operational complexity.
examples: Unfortunately, I'm not able to apply discount codes retrospectively to orders that have already been placed.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks to apply a recently-expired discount to their order.

policy_rules.description: Free shipping is only available for orders meeting the minimum spend threshold. Cannot be applied to orders below threshold.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When customer asks for free shipping on orders below the threshold amount.
policy_rules.constraints: No exceptions. Free shipping is a promotional tool, not a negotiable service.
examples: Our free shipping threshold is [amount], and I'm not able to adjust the shipping cost for orders below that amount.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks for free shipping on order below minimum spend.

policy_rules.description: Preorder shipping dates are estimates, not guarantees. Delays may occur due to production realities.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When proactively updating preorder customers about delays or responding to timeline inquiries.
policy_rules.constraints: Must be transparent about potential delays. Offer cancellation option if timeline is unacceptable.
examples: We're currently expecting this to ship by [revised date], though please understand this is an estimate and may shift slightly as production completes.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when managing preorder expectations about shipping timelines.

policy_rules.description: Sagitine products are warranted against structural defects. Does not cover normal wear, user modification, or damage from misuse.
policy_rules.when_to_apply: When determining if a free replacement is warranted outside standard return window.
policy_rules.constraints: Warranty covers manufacturing defects, not user damage or modifications. Assessment required for each case.
examples: Could you please provide a photo of the issue? I'll need to assess whether this is covered under our warranty.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer requests replacement outside standard return window and warranty status is unclear.

product_knowledge.topic: Storage Boxes vs. Drawers - Design Philosophy
product_knowledge.snippet: Sagitine Boxes are designed as modular storage boxes that sit within a frame, rather than sliding drawers on runners. They are intended to be completely removed using two hands for organization and arranging. This design choice provides flexibility to reorganise storage over time and protect pieces by keeping them enclosed when not in use.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when explaining why Sagitine products don't function like traditional drawers.

product_knowledge.snippet: The boxes are built from a premium paper-based, hand-wrapped construction. They should never be glued, adhered, taped, or otherwise modified, as this causes significant structural and visual damage. Any form of adhesion compromises the paper surface and cannot be undone.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks about gluing boxes or making modifications to improve fit.

product_knowledge.snippet: Assembly of a Sagitine Stand is minimal and straightforward, requiring only an Allen key (included) and a screwdriver. Instructions are provided by email before delivery and physically in the box. Assembly time is typically 5-10 minutes. Can be assembled by one person. Sturdy construction once assembled.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks about assembly difficulty or missing instructions.

product_knowledge.snippet: Sagitine Boxes are finished with premium materials. Care guidelines: Wipe clean with dry, soft cloth. Avoid harsh chemicals or abrasives. Not suitable for dishwasher cleaning. For timber products, use food-safe timber oil quarterly to maintain finish. Maintain in dry environment away from direct heat sources. Not waterproof - avoid liquid cleaners.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks how to clean or care for their Sagitine products.

product_knowledge.snippet: Inserts are designed exclusively to sit within a relevant Sagitine Box format to organize specific wardrobe and lifestyle items. They are not intended or designed to be utilized as standalone pieces. Inserts require the Box structure to function properly.
correct_wording_patterns: sit within the relevant Sagitine Box

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks if inserts can be used separately from Boxes.

product_knowledge.snippet: Sagitine Boxes are designed with a precise fit within the Stand frame. This intentional tightness prevents Boxes from shifting during use, maintains clean aesthetic, and ensures secure fit. When new, fit can feel particularly snug but typically relaxes slightly with regular use as Boxes settle. Using two hands (one to stabilise Stand, one to lift Box) makes interaction smoother.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer complains that Boxes are too tight or difficult to remove.

product_knowledge.snippet: Sagitine Boxes are designed as enclosed storage boxes rather than open drawers, providing excellent dust protection. The minimal gap between Box and frame is intentional for: air circulation to prevent moisture buildup, easy removal without friction, and flexibility to remove Boxes completely. For long-term storage, garment bags within Boxes provide extra protection layer.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when customer asks about dust protection or notices the gap between Box and frame.

product_knowledge.snippet: Sagitine Boxes use premium paper-based, hand-wrapped construction for distinctive aesthetic and lightweight feel. This requires specific care: avoid water or liquid cleaners (wipe with dry soft cloth only), no adhesive/tape/glue (damages paper surface), keep away from direct heat or high humidity. Timber components need food-safe timber oil quarterly. This premium construction is part of Sagitine's character - with proper care, pieces will last for years.

retrieval_metadata.confidence_signals: Use when explaining product construction, care requirements, or why modifications are not recommended.

core_tone.calm: Composed and measured, never reactive or defensive
core_tone.warm: Friendly and personal without being gushy or familiar
core_tone.polished: Elevated language without being corporate or sterile
core_tone.quietly_premium: Understated confidence, not loud or salesy
core_tone.never_corporate: No jargon, no legalese, no 'team' deflections

preferred_phrasing: Thank you for letting me know
Just let me know what works best

structure.direct_next_step: Always include clear next action or question
structure.concise_explanation: Provide just enough context, not over-explanation
structure.elegant_sign_off: Always end with 'Warm regards, Heidi x'

operational_constraints.no_apology_drift: Never say 'I'm sorry' - replace with 'Thank you for letting me know' or 'Thank you for bringing this to my attention'
operational_constraints.no_deflection: Don't say 'we' or 'the team' - use 'I' and take ownership
operational_constraints.no_over_promise: Never promise timelines or outcomes you can't guarantee
operational_constraints.no_policy_invention: Don't invent policies or goodwill - stay within documented rules
operational_constraints.no_modified_product: Never suggest home modifications (wax, runners, glue) to address product characteristics

risk_escalation.high_risk_action: Immediate escalation to management, no direct customer response
risk_escalation.medium_risk_action: Senior review before response, de-escalation focus

scenario_tags.description: Item was damaged during the shipping/courier process.
Order is missing assembly hardware or key structural pieces.
Customer misunderstands how the product is originally intended to function.
Customer is based in Sydney and qualifies for/requests local assembled vehicle delivery options.
Customer prefers a money back refund rather than waiting for a replacement part.
Polite rejection of an influencer, press, or wholesale inquiry.
Tracking shows no movement beyond expected delivery window.
Tracking shows 'Delivered' but customer claims they did not receive package.
Customer confirms package was stolen from their property after delivery.
Customer entered wrong address at checkout and order has been dispatched.
Package is in transit but moving more slowly than expected.
Customer received only part of their order (multiple cartons shipped).
Customer asks to apply recently-ended promotion to their order.
Discount code did not work at checkout.
Customer asks for free shipping on order below minimum spend.
Customer wants to buy individual Boxes or Stands separately.
Customer language indicates frustration, anger, or escalation.
scenario_tags.risk_level: High (risk of negative review or further escalation)

scenario_tags.description: Product doesn't meet customer expectations.
Customer complains Boxes are too tight or difficult to remove.
Customer asks about dust protection or notices gap between Box and frame.
Assembly instructions were not in the package.
Customer needs replacement parts but doesn't specify what.
Customer wants to return a Stand requiring courier pickup.
Customer asks if they can pick up order locally (especially Sydney).
Notifying waitlist customers that out-of-stock items are now available.
Preorder shipping timeline has extended or customer is asking about timing.
scenario_tags.riskency: Medium (customer may cancel if wait is too long)

gaps_remaining.description: No documented clear policy for handling duties, taxes, or international shipping delays. Critical for any future cross-border expansion.
gaps_remaining.blocker: Not a blocker for domestic go-live, but should be documented before international expansion.

gaps_remaining.description: Warranty term (e.g., 1 year, 2 years) is not explicitly called out in templates. Makes it vague when deciding if free replacement is warranted outside standard return window.
gaps_remaining.blocker: Partial blocker - warranty rule exists but lacks specific timeframe. Recommend documenting 12-month structural warranty.

gaps_remaining.description: No clear guidance on when to offer goodwill vs when not to. Current approach is to decline most requests, which may miss opportunities to build loyalty in edge cases.
gaps_remaining.blocker: Not a blocker for go-live, but goodwill framework should be developed for customer retention strategy.

gaps_remaining.description: No special handling or recognition for repeat customers or high LTV customers. All customers treated the same regardless of lifetime value.
gaps_remaining.blocker: Not a blocker - enhancement opportunity for future CX personalisation.

gaps_remaining.description: No specific templates or protocols for when customer issues surface via social media channels (Instagram, TikTok) rather than email.
gaps_remaining.blocker: Not a blocker for email-first go-live, but should be documented before social media CX integration.
